name: Homespun Linen Shirt
description: A plain cream linen shirt with full sleeves and gathered cuffs, suitable for everyday wear among laborers, apprentices, and colonists in the Atlantic world.

name: Harbor Poet Shirt
description: A loose-cut beige shirt with an open collar and billowing sleeves, the sort of practical but stylish garment a town clerk, sailor, or wandering writer might wear.

name: Merchant Waistcoat Shirt
description: A white linen shirt layered beneath a brown waistcoat, fit for a shopkeeper, trader, or prosperous colonial merchant conducting business in port towns.

name: Governor's Cravat Shirt
description: An elegant ivory shirt with pleated front panels, ruffled cuffs, and a formal cravat, suitable for a gentleman of rank or an official at a colonial gathering.

name: Mariner's Neckerchief Shirt
description: A worn cream work shirt tied with a blue neckerchief, ideal for a sailor, dockhand, or traveler moving between ship and shore.

name: Sailor's Smock
description: A cream maritime smock with a navy sailor collar and necktie, inspired by seafaring dress and well suited to Atlantic crossings and naval life.

name: Fisherman's Work Smock
description: A rugged blue work smock with large patch pockets and rope ties, built for fishermen, harbor workers, and labor in wet coastal conditions.

name: Buckskin Fringe Shirt
description: A tan buckskin shirt with fringe and leather ties, inspired by frontier and Indigenous-influenced clothing traditions adapted to travel and hunting.

name: Burgundy Brocade Doublet
description: A richly colored burgundy garment with brocade texture, gold-trim accents, and billowing sleeves, suited to a wealthy patron, courtly figure, or theatrical merchant.

name: Ruffled Dress Shirt
description: A crisp white shirt with ruffled chest detailing and lace-trimmed cuffs, reflecting the more formal fashions worn by gentlemen on ceremonial or social occasions.
